exercises.name: Pilates - Teaser I
exercises.description: Lie on your back, lift legs to 45 degrees, roll up to balance on your sit bones with arms extended forward.

exercises.name: Pilates - Teaser II
exercises.description: Perform Teaser I but focus on slower movement control as you lower back down.

exercises.name: Pilates - Teaser III
exercises.description: Lift both legs and upper body together into a V-sit position, balancing on your sit bones.

exercises.name: Pilates - Teaser with Ball Between Knees
exercises.description: Lie on your back and hold a ball between your knees. Engage your core and lift into a teaser position.

exercises.name: Pilates - The Hundred
exercises.description: Lie on your back, lift your head, neck, and shoulders, extend legs at 45 degrees. Pump arms up and down while inhaling for five counts and exhaling for five counts, totaling 100 pumps.

exercises.name: Pilates - Toe Taps with Ball Under Lower Back
exercises.description: Lie on your back with a small ball under your lower back. Tap one foot to the mat at a time while maintaining core control.

exercises.name: Pilates - Twist (Side Plank to twist)
exercises.description: Hold a side plank, rotate your torso, and thread the top arm under your body before returning.

exercises.name: Pilates - Walkout to Push up
exercises.alternateName: Push up with ball under chest
exercises.description: Perform a Pilates push-up with a ball under your chest for added challenge.

exercises.name: Pilates - Wide Second plié - Alternating Heel Raises
exercises.description: In a deep plié, lift one heel off the floor, then lower and switch to the other heel. Keep the body stable and thighs engaged throughout.

exercises.name: Pilates - Wide Second plié - Full Range
exercises.description: Stand with feet wide and toes turned out. Lower into a deep plié, then return to standing.

exercises.name: Pilates - Wide Second plié - Pulses
exercises.description: From the deep plié position, stay low and pulse up and down in small, controlled movements. Maintain tension through the thighs and glutes.

exercises.name: Pilates - Wide second plié to split lunge
exercises.description: Start in a wide plié squat. Pivot on your feet and drop into a split lunge, then return to center and repeat on the other side. Use your core to stabilize the transition.

exercises.name: Pilates Ball Bird Dog Tricep Pulse
exercises.description:
Start in a bird dog position on the floor: hands and knees with your back flat, core engaged, and place one hand on top of a Pilates ball. 

Extend the leg opposite to the hand on the ball straight behind you. Perform a single-arm tricep pulse with the hand on the ball by bending the elbow slightly and pressing down toward the ball in small controlled movements.

exercises.name: Pilates Ball Bird Dog Tricep Push Up
exercises.description:
Start in a bird dog position on the floor: hands and knees with your back flat, core engaged, and place one hand on top of a Pilates ball. 

Extend the leg opposite to the hand on the ball straight behind you. Perform a single-arm tricep push-up with the hand on the ball by bending the elbow and lowering your chest toward the ball, then pressing back up.

exercises.name: Pilates Ball Crunch with Rotation
exercises.description:
Lie on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat. Place a Pilates ball under your upper back. Place your hands behind your head for support, elbows wide. 

Engage your core and perform a crunch with rotation by lifting your upper back off the ball and twisting your torso to one side only, bringing your elbow toward the opposite knee. 

Lower back down under control to the starting position and repeat for reps, keeping the movement on the same side throughout.

exercises.name: Pilates Ball Pick Ups
exercises.description:
Start in a bear position: hands and feet on the floor, hips lifted, knees hovering just above the ground, and core engaged. 

Place a Pilates ball on the floor in front of you. Using your knees, lift or pick up the ball from the floor, then drop it back down. 

Repeat this pick-up and drop motion for the desired number of reps while keeping your upper body stable and core engaged throughout.

exercises.name: Pilates Ball Weighted Plie Squats
exercises.description:
Stand on a Pilates ball with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and toes pointed outward. 

Hold a dumbbell with both hands close to your chest in a goblet position. Lower your hips down into a plie squat, keeping your chest up and core engaged, while maintaining balance on the ball. 

Push through your heels to return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Pilates Ball Weighted Pulse
exercises.description:
Lie on your back on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat. Place a Pilates ball under your upper back. 

Hold a dumbbell with both hands extended toward your knees. Engage your core and perform small pulsing crunch-like movements, lifting your upper back slightly off the ball as if trying to sit up, then lowering back down under control. 

Keep your lower back lightly pressed to the floor and focus on using your abs to drive the movement.

exercises.name: Pilates bent knee crunch w/ adduction
exercises.description: Squeeze a yoga block, ball or a rolled towel between knees to activate pelvic floor muscles.

exercises.name: Pilates crunch w/ alt leg raises
exercises.description: Keep core tight. Crunch up and pull your belly button into your spine. “Hold your pee” contraction and slowly lower one leg at a time. You do not need to lower your legs all the way to the floor. Do these reps slowly and keep your core engaged. Take breaks if you need to between reps. You can increase difficulty by securing a band behind you, and holding handles in position to add resistance.

exercises.name: Pilates crunch w/ pelvic abduction
exercises.description: Crunch up, keeping core tight, “ hold your pee”. Pull legs up into “ birth “ position and slowly release out and pull back in. Do not add a band. The focus is on INNER thighs and pelvic muscles. Let your legs drop out as far as they can and pull them back in. The farther down they drop the more challenging this exercise will be. (As you can see I could use some stretching )

exercises.name: Pilates crunch with bent knee adduction
exercises.description: I want you to keep your core tight, "hold your pee" contraction and let your legs fall out slowly and pull them back in. This is an inner thigh exercise so please do not add a band. That will work abduction and not adduction. The farther your knees fall out the better of a "burn" you will feel. Keep abdominals pulled in towards your spine. It should almost be difficult to breath. If you can only perform 5-6 reps , take a quick breath and continue.

exercises.name: Pilates low leg lifts with yoga block foot squeeze
Pilates Oblique Crunch with Leg Raise (Lv 3)
exercises.description: Pilates Oblique Crunch with Leg Raise (Lv 3)

exercises.name: Pilates pull over w/ knee add bridge
